_description: Golden expected output for fidelity_history_golden.csv. Pinned contract for any Fidelity transaction parser (Python or Keepfolio TS). If totals diverge, the parser has regressed.

rows._test_intent: Plain DIRECT DEPOSIT — base wages case. Employer name extracted from Action.
Standard DIVIDEND RECEIVED — base investment-income case.

DIVIDEND RECEIVED SUBSTITUTE PAYMENT — verifies the more-specific pattern is checked BEFORE plain DIVIDEND RECEIVED. If the match order is wrong, this row still lands in INCOME_DIVIDEND by accident, so the assertion is on category PLUS the marker text in the action — see test_substitute_payment_pattern_priority.
REINVESTMENT — must NOT count as income. The cash was already booked under the matching DIVIDEND RECEIVED row. Double-counting here was the Q1 2026 analysis bug.
JOURNALED (cash <-> margin sweep) — net economic effect zero. MUST NOT inflate income totals.

DIRECT DEBIT APPLECARD — credit card payoff. The merchant-level spending is captured on the CC statement. Counting this as spending double-counts. This was the cause of the initial $38,842/mo (142% of income) error in Q1 2026.
DEBIT CARD PURCHASE H-E-B — real spending. Merchant name in Action, NOT Description (which says 'No Description'). Sub-category resolves via EXPENSE_PATTERNS.

_excluded_from_both._note: These are listed separately for diagnostic visibility. They are NOT income and NOT spending. A parser that includes any of them in either total has regressed.
